message:
🐛 Fix Resource Marshall workload resolution errors

Root cause: useWorkloads(undefined) fetched ALL 678 workloads across
clusters when no cluster/namespace was selected. Users could see stale
workloads from other clusters in the dropdown and select workloads that
didn't exist in the chosen cluster/namespace, causing "not found" errors.

Fixes:
- Add enabled parameter to useWorkloads; ResourceMarshall passes
  enabled=false until both cluster and namespace are selected
- Clear stale data in useWorkloads when options change
- Improve backend error handling: distinguish "not found" from
  connectivity/RBAC errors using apierrors.IsNotFound

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
